Guideline: Content Layer API replaces legacy collections
Description: Legacy content collections (src/content/config.ts, type: content, entry.render(), entry.slug) are fully removed. Must migrate to Content Layer API
Do: Migrate to Content Layer API with defineCollection() and loaders; use legacy.collectionsBackwardsCompat temporarily if needed
Don't: Keep using legacy content collections - they will fail to build in v6
Code Bad:
// Legacy config.ts (removed)
const blog = defineCollection({ type: 'content', schema: z.object({...}) });

Guideline: Import Zod from astro/zod not astro:content
Description: Zod 4 is included in Astro 6. Import z from astro/zod. The old astro:schema and z from astro:content imports are deprecated
Do: Use import { z } from 'astro/zod'
Don't: Import z from astro:content or use astro:schema

Description: Content collections reload instantly in dev mode without a full page refresh. No rebuild needed for content changes
Do: Use getCollection() and getEntry() as before; live reload works automatically with Content Layer API
Don't: Manually restart dev server on every content change
Code Good: Nothing to change - just use Content Layer API
Code Bad: Restarting astro dev after every .md edit

Description: Defer dynamic sections of a page to render at request time while the rest ships as static HTML from CDN
Do: Add server:defer to components needing runtime data (pricing, inventory); always provide a fallback slot
Don't: Apply server:defer to static components or omit the fallback slot
Code Good: Loading pricing...

Description: Configure cache headers per Server Island: pricing may need no-store while reviews can use max-age=300 with stale-while-revalidate
Do: Set cache control headers per island based on data freshness requirements
Don't: Use the same cache policy for all islands regardless of data volatility

Description: ViewTransitions is fully removed in v6. Use ClientRouter for SPA-like page transitions with prefetch on hover and DOM morphing
Do: Import { ClientRouter } from 'astro:transitions' and add to your head or layout
Don't: Keep using ViewTransitions - it no longer exists in v6
Code Good:
import { ClientRouter } from 'astro:transitions';

<head><ClientRouter /></head>

Guideline: Prefetch on hover for instant navigation
Description: ClientRouter prefetches linked pages on hover or when they enter the viewport, making navigation feel instant
Do: Enable ClientRouter and all internal links get prefetching automatically
Don't: Add manual prefetch logic on top of ClientRouter
Code Good: Nothing extra needed - ClientRouter handles prefetch automatically

Description: Page transitions must work without JavaScript. ClientRouter enhances navigation but content must be accessible with standard MPA navigation
Do: Ensure all pages work as standard MPA navigation without ClientRouter JavaScript
Don't: Build features that only work with ClientRouter JS
Code Good:
<ClientRouter />
<!-- All pages still work as normal MPA navigation -->
Code Bad: Relying on transition animations for content to appear

Description: The Fonts API downloads Google Fonts at build time, stores them locally, generates fallback metrics, and adds preload hints
Do: Use Font component from astro:fonts with provider=google
Don't: Use link tags to Google Fonts CDN when the built-in API exists
Code Good:
import { Font } from 'astro:fonts';
<Font provider=google family=Inter weights={[400, 600, 700]} />

Guideline: Local font files with automatic optimization
Description: The Fonts API handles local font files: detects formats (woff2, ttf), generates fallback metrics, and creates optimized subsets
Do: Use provider=local for custom fonts stored in your project
Don't: Load local fonts with raw @font-face declarations when the API exists
Code Good: <Font provider=local src=./fonts/custom.woff2 family=CustomFont />

Description: Astro 6 provides stable CSP configuration via security.csp in astro.config. Auto-hashes all scripts and styles
Do: Configure security.csp in astro.config.mjs with directives object
Don't: Add CSP headers manually via middleware or server config
Code Bad: Manual CSP header injection in server middleware

Description: Astro 6 uses Vite 7. The dev server runs the exact production runtime during development using the Environments API
Do: Update any Vite plugins to be compatible with Vite 7 if they access internal APIs
Don't: Assume Vite 4 or 5 plugin compatibility without checking
Code Good: Check your Vite plugins are Vite 7 compatible
Code Bad: Using outdated Vite plugins without checking compatibility

Description: z.string().email() is now z.email(); .default() must match output type; error keys use error instead of message
Do: Migrate Zod schemas to Zod 4 syntax; check the Zod migration guide
Don't: Fix Zod errors one by one at build time

Description: Astro 6 drops Node 18 and 20 support entirely. Dev and production must run Node 22.12.0 or later
Do: Update package.json engines to node >=22.12.0 and update CI/CD environments
Don't: Deploy to platforms still running Node 18 or 20

Description: Astro 6 provides legacy.collectionsBackwardsCompat as a temporary migration flag. Each collection must be migrated individually to Content Layer API
Do: Use the backwards compat flag as a stopgap while migrating collections one at a time
Don't: Stay on the backwards compat flag permanently - it may be removed in a future major version

Description: Use build.assetsPrefix from astro:config/server instead of import.meta.env.ASSETS_PREFIX
Do: Import assetsPrefix from astro:config/server
Don't: Reference import.meta.env.ASSETS_PREFIX directly
Code Good:
import { build } from 'astro:config/server';
const prefix = build.assetsPrefix;

Description: .cjs and .cts config files are no longer supported. Use .mjs, .js, .ts, or .mts for astro.config
Do: Rename any .cjs or .cts config files to .mjs or .ts
Don't: Keep CommonJS config files or require() calls
Code Good: astro.config.mjs or astro.config.ts

Description: Number params in getStaticPaths are no longer allowed. Convert all params values to strings explicitly
Do: Use String(id) or template literals to convert params to strings
Don't: Return number values in params

Description: Calling getImage() on the client now throws a runtime error. Image optimization is server-only
Do: Call getImage() only in frontmatter or server-side code; pass results to client components as props
Don't: Use getImage() in client-side event handlers or useEffect
Code Good:
---
const optimized = await getImage({ src: img, width: 800 });
---
<ImageComponent src={optimized.src} />

Guideline: Responsive image styles use data attributes
Description: Astro emits responsive image styles via hash classes and data-* attributes instead of inline styles for CSP compatibility
Do: Update CSS selectors targeting image inline styles to use data-astro-image selector
Don't: Target inline style attributes on Astro images with CSS selectors
Code Good: img[data-astro-image]

Description: The redirectToDefaultLocale config option now defaults to false to prevent infinite redirect loops
Do: Explicitly set redirectToDefaultLocale: true if you rely on automatic locale redirects
Don't: Assume users get redirected to default locale without explicit config

Description: A new Rust-based .astro compiler offers faster build times with stronger diagnostics. Enable with rustCompiler flag
Do: Enable for faster builds if you have many .astro files; test thoroughly before production use
Don't: Enable in production without testing all pages
Code Bad: Not enabled (Go compiler used by default)

Description: A two-pass rendering strategy with up to 2x faster rendering. Planned as default for Astro v7
Do: Try on build-heavy projects; compare build times with and without the flag
Don't: Enable without measuring impact
Code Good: experimental: { queuedRendering: true }

Description: Platform-agnostic API for caching SSR responses using web-standard cache semantics. Integrates with Live Content Collections for auto-invalidation
Do: Use for SSR pages with cacheable content; configure per-route cache policies
Don't: Cache personalized content or pages with user-specific data

Description: Syntax highlighting upgraded to Shiki 4. Most cases work automatically; check migration guide for custom themes
Do: Test your code blocks render correctly after upgrade
Don't: Pin to Shiki 3 or ignore highlighting regressions
Code Good: Default Shiki config works for most cases
Code Bad: Custom Shiki themes without v4 compatibility check

Guideline: Sharp for image processing and SVG rasterization
Description: Astro 6 defaults to Sharp and rasterizes SVGs to PNG/WebP automatically. Also never upscales images by default
Do: Let Sharp handle image optimization; pre-upscale manually if needed
Don't: Expect SVGs to stay as SVGs when requesting raster formats
Code Good: Nothing needed for most users
Code Bad: Relying on SVG format when requesting PNG output

Guideline: Hybrid rendering for mixed static and SSR
Description: Use output: hybrid to render most pages statically and opt into SSR per-page with export const prerender = false
Do: Default to static; add prerender = false only to pages that need server rendering
Don't: Use output: server when most pages could be static
Code Bad: output: 'server' for a mostly-static marketing site

Description: The old experimental.failOnPrerenderConflict is removed. Use the stable prerenderConflictBehavior option (error, warn, or ignore)
Do: Set prerenderConflictBehavior: 'error' to catch prerender conflicts during build
Don't: Ignore prerender conflicts without any config
Code Good: prerenderConflictBehavior: 'error'
